mnemonics.title: DP Formula Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: Remember: DP[i] = max(EXCLUDE, INCLUDE) where EXCLUDE = DP[i-1] and INCLUDE = weight[i] + DP[p(i)] - think 'Don't take it or take it, whichever makes you greater'.

mnemonics.title: p(i) Meaning
mnemonics.content: p(i) stands for 'predecessor' - the rightmost interval that finishes before i starts. If no predecessor exists, p(i) = 0.

acronyms.usage: The optimization problem of selecting non-overlapping intervals to maximize total weight
The technique used to solve WISP by building solutions from subproblems
Index of rightmost compatible interval finishing before interval i starts

visualTips.visualization: Think of a staircase where each step i represents considering interval i. At each step, you either take the step (add its weight plus all compatible steps before) or skip it (keep previous best).
Imagine a timeline with intervals as bars. For interval i, p(i) is the closest bar that ends before i starts - use binary search on end times like finding the floor value.
Two intervals are compatible if they don't touch - draw them as non-overlapping horizontal bars on a timeline.

analogies.analogy: Like deciding whether to add a premium item to your shopping cart - either you skip it (keep previous total) or take it (add its value plus what was compatible before).
Like finding your most recent previous class that finished before your current class started - the most recent compatible event.
Unweighted is like counting how many books you can fit on a shelf. Weighted is like maximizing the total value of books you fit, where each book has a price tag.
